steps.title: Use the perimeter scale factor
steps.content: For similar triangles, corresponding side lengths and perimeters use the same scale factor. Compare the two perimeters first, then apply that factor to the corresponding side.

steps.title: Find the scale factor from the perimeters
steps.content: For similar triangles, corresponding sides and perimeters use the same scale factor. The table gives perimeter $50$ for triangle $TUV$ and $150$ for triangle $XYZ$, so the scale factor from $TUV$ to $XYZ$ is $$\frac{150}{50}=3.$$

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to find XY
steps.content: $TU$ corresponds to $XY$, and $TU=6$. Multiply by the scale factor: $$XY=6\cdot3=18.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The corresponding side length is $18$, so C is correct.
